reasoning: The flagged scripts (setup_rke2.sh, setup_app.sh) are infrastructure/deployment tools not executed during AUR build/install. They pose no risk to end-users during `makepkg` operations.
disagreement_analysis: qwen model misinterpreted deployment scripts as malicious due to credential handling, while deepseek correctly contextualized them as non-executable during build.
coverage_issues: Both models missed reviewing .install hooks if present, but none exist in package
false_positives: setup_rke2.sh: Legitimate cluster setup; GitHub secret storage is intentional deployment pattern
setup_app.sh: SSH key generation for app deployment is expected behavior, not credential theft
learnings: Improve context awareness: Models must better distinguish build-time vs deployment scripts
Enhance file prioritization: Rust source/build.rs should be higher priority than infrastructure scripts
Clarify scope rules: Explicitly flag non-build files (CI/deployment) as out-of-scope in prompts
